teaser: Discover what Eric Lee prioritized for 2020 and how he approached setting meaningful goals at the start of the decade.
Learn why pursuing flawless execution might be sabotaging your progress and how embracing your messy, unfinished work could be the breakthrough you've been waiting for.

Eric Lee explores how the most effective leaders know when to push forward with conviction and when to step back and let their teams lead, examining the tension between maintaining momentum and building the psychological safety that enables genuine trust.

Discover how grouping similar tasks into focused blocks can transform your productivity from scattered to unstoppable, turning your work day into a coordinated dance rather than a chaotic shuffle. Learn the psychology behind why batching works and the specific frameworks that separate people who actually finish projects from those who just start them.
Discover how the most transformative mentorships operate less like formal arrangements and more like unexpected gifts that reshape your entire trajectory.
Discover why your protective barriers might be holding you back more than they're helping, and what it actually takes to emerge into your fullest potential.

Discover how your sense of calling shapes not just your career, but your entire identity—and why true stewardship means living that calling as a daily practice, not just a job title. Eric Lee explores the unexpected connections between vocation, self-understanding, and responsible living in this reflective piece.

Spotify's dominance isn't just about superior technology—it's about genuinely hearing what listeners want and adapting faster than competitors who were too busy talking about their own visions. Discover how intentional listening became the streaming giant's competitive edge.

Leaders who fail to communicate outcomes leave their teams trapped in uncertainty, wondering if their work actually mattered. Discover why closing the loop—completing the cycle from delegation to feedback—is the difference between building trust and breeding resentment.
Eric Lee reflects on what it means to deliberately end something meaningful and how closure—both intentional and unexpected—shapes who we become.

Discover how separating urgent tasks from important ones can transform your productivity and help you reclaim focus on what truly matters. The Eisenhower Box reveals why most people spend their days fighting fires instead of building their future.

Before you can chase your goals, you need to know what winning actually looks like—and for most people, success is defined by everyone except themselves. Discover how reframing your definition of success can free you from the expectations that were never yours to begin with.

When you can't resist tweaking every detail of a delegated task, you're not leading—you're just doing two jobs poorly. Learn why letting go of control is the only way to build a team that actually scales beyond you.
Explore how small, consistent spiritual practices can reshape your perspective and ground you when life feels chaotic, drawing lessons from the author's own Saturday morning rituals.
Discover how the gap between what you imagine and what actually happens shapes your decisions—and why closing that distance might be simpler than you think.

Sorting through your messy first drafts and half-baked ideas might feel unproductive, but that creative chaos is exactly where breakthroughs happen. Learn why embracing the stumbling, nonlinear journey of making something—rather than obsessing over the finished product—is what separates prolific creators from perpetual procrastinators.
Discover how the Enneagram's nine personality types reveal hidden patterns in how we sacrifice ourselves—and whether those sacrifices actually serve our growth or merely reinforce our deepest fears.
Discover why simply accumulating years of experience or cramming your brain with information isn't enough—and what the crucial missing ingredient is that transforms both into genuine wisdom.

Discover how genuine religious liberty differs from the weaponization of faith in modern politics—and why defending one doesn't require abandoning the other. Eric Lee explores the uncomfortable tensions between personal conviction and collective freedom in this week's Sensible Saturdays.

Discover why showing up consistently in small, unglamorous moments might be the most radical act of faith in a world obsessed with grand gestures and viral breakthroughs. Eric Lee explores how faithfulness rewires our understanding of what it truly means to live out our deepest beliefs.
When work bleeds into every corner of your life, sometimes the only way to reset is to step back completely—discover why fasting and intentional retreat might be the antidote to always-on burnout.

Discover how removing constraints from the learning process—whether imposed by institutions, curricula, or self-imposed limitations—unlocks deeper understanding and genuine intellectual curiosity. Eric Lee explores why the most transformative education often happens when we stop being told what to learn and start pursuing knowledge on our own terms.

When opportunity arrives, most people hesitate—but the winners are those bold enough to pursue it immediately, even before they feel completely ready. Learn why waiting for perfect conditions is the enemy of progress, and how to recognize when the moment to act has finally arrived.

When God's sovereignty and human freedom collide in the doctrine of grace, which one wins—or do they dance together in a tension that defies resolution? Eric Lee explores how predestination and free will aren't opponents but partners in an ancient theological mystery that still divides churches today.

When your guests arrive, the small gestures—a warm greeting at the door, remembering how they take their coffee, clearing space for their coats—transform an ordinary gathering into an experience where people feel genuinely seen and valued. Discover how intentional hospitality becomes the foundation of deeper relationships and more memorable moments together.

Dongles reveal an unexpected lesson about the trade-offs between convenience and hidden costs that shape our daily tech decisions. Discover what a simple cable connector teaches us about the habits we've grown to accept without questioning.

Discover why your worth isn't determined by your accomplishments or failures, but by something far more profound that transforms how you see yourself and others. Eric Lee explores the radical shift that happens when you truly understand God's perspective on your identity.
Discover how to absorb transformative shifts without fracturing your identity, and why the most resilient people treat change as something to integrate rather than merely survive.

Understanding your audience's deepest desires and fears—like Hades interrogating souls at the gates of the underworld—is the ultimate riddle that separates forgettable messages from ones that resonate. Discover how to crack this code and speak directly to what your audience truly needs to hear.
Discover why the most productive learners aren't those who push themselves hardest, but those who strategically recognize when to pause and consolidate what they've already absorbed.
Discover why Eric Lee believes some promises aren't meant to be broken—and what it takes to honor them when everything around you is screaming to let go.

When external circumstances shift and your carefully laid plans crumble, a compass—your core values and principles—becomes more reliable than any map ever could be. Learn why adaptability rooted in clarity about what truly matters is the difference between rigidity that breaks and resilience that bends.

When you're caught in the weeds of daily chaos, zooming out reveals the patterns and opportunities that your immediate perspective can't see—and Eric Lee explains why this shift in focus might be exactly what your strategy needs.
Discover how to cultivate inner stillness when life's biggest changes feel most overwhelming, drawing on practical wisdom that bridges the gap between acceptance and forward momentum.

The Golden Rule tells you to treat others as you'd want to be treated, but what if their needs are fundamentally different from yours? Discover why adapting your approach to how *others* actually want to be treated might be the real secret to better relationships and influence.

Discover how one typing attack forced a fundamental reckoning about whether you're filling your own cup or depleting it in service of others. Eric Lee explores the paradox of pouring out when you haven't been poured into.
Discover what makes certain prayers transform lives while others seem to fall on deaf ears, and learn the specific practices that unlock prayer's true power.
Most people confess their values while their calendars reveal their true priorities—discover how to align the two and stop letting default decisions hijack your life.

Discover how the "jar of tasks" metaphor reveals why your to-do list keeps overflowing—and the counterintuitive prioritization method that actually lets you fit what matters most. Learn the bonus technique that separates people who accomplish their goals from those who just stay busy.

Most people stop at symptoms when solving problems, but discovering the actual root cause requires asking the right questions—and having the courage to follow the trail wherever it leads. Learn the framework that separates band-aid fixes from lasting solutions.

Isaiah Berlin's ancient parable suggests that foxes know many things while hedgehogs know one big thing—and it turns out that having a singular, crystalline sense of purpose might be the competitive advantage nobody's talking about. Rather than chasing every opportunity that glimmers, the most fulfilled people seem to have identified their central organizing principle and built their entire lives around it.

Friction isn't just an obstacle—it's often the invisible force keeping you stuck in habits, relationships, and systems that don't serve you. Learn how to identify where unnecessary resistance lives in your life and redesign your environment so the path of least resistance actually leads somewhere worth going.

As 2019 drew to a close, Eric Lee took stock of the year's pivotal moments and lessons that reshaped his perspective on work, creativity, and purpose. Discover what stood out most from his annual reflection and why he believes the insights matter heading into the new decade.

When stress lingers despite your best efforts to manage it, the problem often isn't your coping strategies—it's that you're treating the symptom instead of the source. Discover why some stress refuses to budge and what actually works to clear it out for good.

Saying no to the wrong opportunities is how you actually say yes to scaling yourself and building something meaningful. Discover why strategic rejection might be the most powerful growth lever you're not using.

Just as you wouldn't expect a warrior to fight without sleep, your productivity won't survive if you don't schedule rest as deliberately as you schedule work. Discover why treating downtime like a calendar commitment—not a luxury—is the only way to actually win against burnout.

The most successful brands don't sell what their product does—they sell the emotional transformation their customers experience. Discover why Apple's marketing genius lies in making you feel like an innovator, not just buying a computer.
Discover how the most meaningful relationships thrive when you can be completely ridiculous together without losing the depth of genuine connection.

Discover how Zoom's meteoric rise from obscurity to household name reveals the power of borrowing ideas from competitors and building on decades of existing technology rather than inventing from scratch. Eric Lee explores why the most successful companies often aren't the most original—they're the ones that execute the borrowed blueprint better than everyone else.

What does it really mean to acknowledge your own privilege, and how can that awareness become a catalyst for dismantling the systems that benefit you at others' expense? Eric Lee explores the uncomfortable but necessary journey of moving from complicity to active grace.

When your capacity to do good work collapses under the weight of too many commitments, the problem isn't your willpower—it's your inability to say no before it's too late. Eric Lee explores why ambitious people often end up stretched thinner than they ever intended, and what actually happens when you finally hit the breaking point.
Discover why surrendering control might be the most strategic decision you'll ever make, and how trusting the process can unlock outcomes you never thought possible.

Rather than waiting for that perfect breakthrough moment, discover how celebrating incremental progress can compound into extraordinary results and rewire your brain to stay motivated. Eric Lee breaks down why the smallest victories are often the hidden foundation of sustainable success.

When you explain a concept to someone else, you're forced to confront the gaps in your own understanding—and that friction is where real learning happens. The act of teaching transforms you as much as it transforms your students.

As productivity culture pushes us toward constant optimization, Eric Lee explores why rest has become radical—and how redefining what's "normal" might be the most productive thing we do. Discover why taking time off isn't laziness; it's a necessary reset for sustainable living.

Discovering your "why" isn't just motivational jargon—it's the foundation that transforms scattered effort into unstoppable momentum and turns ordinary goals into compelling missions worth pursuing relentlessly.

Discover what happens when you stop performing for an invisible audience and start communicating what actually matters to you. Eric Lee explores the gap between the self we project and the self we authentically express.
Discover how the pursuit of understanding across disciplines creates unexpected connections that transform both individual growth and collective progress.

When a royal riddle about infidelity surfaces online, it forces us to confront uncomfortable truths about trust and deception in relationships we thought we understood. This week's one thing explores the puzzle that's making everyone question what they really know about commitment.

Discover the hidden anxieties and unresolved questions that plague high performers, and learn why insomnia might be your mind's way of protecting you from uncomfortable truths. Eric Lee explores the difference between productive worry and destructive rumination in this bonus edition.

The most successful people aren't self-made—they've assembled a carefully curated circle of mentors, peers, and advisors who challenge, support, and guide them through every major decision. Who sits on your personal board, and are they the right people to get you where you want to go?

Public vulnerability isn't a weakness—it's the antidote to perfectionism that keeps most creators paralyzed. Eric Lee explores how sharing unfinished work builds better art, deeper connections, and a thriving creative practice.

When your energy is running on empty, bartering your skills might be the unconventional solution that saves both your wallet and your sanity. Discover how trading what you're good at can break the exhaustion cycle without adding another financial burden to your plate.

When faith feels impossible and circumstances demand your despair, discover how the ancient practice of praising God in darkness becomes not just an act of defiance, but a radical realignment of what you actually believe.

Discover which of your three distinct business personas—the Visionary, the Operator, or the Connector—is dominating your decision-making, and learn why recognizing all three is crucial for sustainable growth. Understanding these competing personalities within you could be the key to finally breaking through your entrepreneurial plateau.
